purpose: Additive relation-ready handoff for Tagalog phrase-detail and listing work without changing runtime truth.

precedenceRules: Phrase text, pronunciation, context, and row status still come from the Tagalog CSV handoff files.
The notes marker only marks which core rows participate in this bounded sample.
This sidecar owns family-level relation edges and advisory next-step or repair guidance.
Parked or deferred follow-ons must stay explicit about status and must not count as promoted clusters.
sourceOfTruth.rowOutcomeLedgerRule: Use the 24-row outcome ledger to retrieve the full prep-only starter, deferred, pickup, and later-only contract without inferring hidden promotion.

relationTypes.description: The target family is a direct reply or acknowledgment to the current family.
The target family is a likely thing the traveler may hear back after saying the current family.
The target family is the most likely next useful traveler move.
The target family is the safer repair branch when the current family is not enough.
The target family is a stronger fallback when the current family still is not enough.
The target family solves a similar need in a nearby context.
The target family is nearby utility worth surfacing without implying strict sequence.

parkedFamilyCandidates.familyTitle: Excuse me, how do I get to this place?
parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: Map-showing stays the first pickup because it closes the biggest current directions gap and routes cleanly back into the live starter direction rows.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Strongest parked row; useful map-showing opener, but it still wants native confidence before promotion.

parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: Reservation stays adjacent to check-in so the next hotel pickup can start from one arrival desk sequence instead of separate backlog rows.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Useful arrival follow-on, but reservation borrowing still needs hotel-language review.

parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: Check-in remains the second arrival-desk move and should keep routing forward to the first room-issue branch instead of floating as an isolated hotel row.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Keep adjacent to reservation work; mixed-English check-in wording still needs review.

parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: The room-issue branch stays in the same pickup cluster so the arrival desk and first in-room problem live on one hotel relation path.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Useful room-issue branch, but aircon borrowing still needs the same hotel-language review lane.

parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: Card payment remains the last next-pass pickup because it is broader than the cash declaration but still needs the same loanword and service-register caution.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Best payment follow-on after the hotel and directions pickup set is resolved.

parkedFamilyCandidates.familyTitle: Could you call them for me?
parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: The escalation branch stays visible for recovery design, but it remains behind the stronger directions, hotel, and card-payment pickup set.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Escalation utility is real, but register and pronoun risk keep it behind the stronger pickup set.

parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: Cash stays as a visible later-only ride follow-on, but it must not outrank the broader card-payment branch.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Narrower than the broader card-payment check, so it stays behind the stronger pickup set.

parkedFamilyCandidates.retrievalNote: The refusal row stays a visible boundary only. If a future detail surface exposes it, it should route back into thanks or the repair lane instead of reading like a promoted starter family.
parkedFamilyCandidates.note: Visible honesty boundary only; keep out of the promoted cluster count until refusal-tone review lands.

statusNormalization.note: Keep real row status and follow-on class separate so prepared-next relation links do not invent hybrid pseudo-status values.

followOnClusters.summary: Ordered pickup cluster covering map-showing first, then hotel arrival and room issue, then card payment.
followOnClusters.unlockRule: Reopen in the listed order only. Keep the five rows adjacent so downstream pickup can recover one travel path instead of a loose parked list.

followOnClusters.summary: Visible later-only hold set for escalation and cash branches that remain behind the stronger pickup cluster.
followOnClusters.unlockRule: Reopen only after the five-row pickup cluster is promoted or explicitly rejected in order.

retrievalContract.rowOutcomeCsvOrder: Rows 1-16 stay starter-core anchors, row 17 stays the deferred refusal boundary, rows 18-22 stay the ordered next-pass pickup cluster, and rows 23-24 stay later-only holds.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: If the refusal lands cleanly, the safest visible close is still a quick thank you.
If the seller keeps explaining or offering things you cannot follow, route back into the direct repair branch.

familyRelations.reason: The refusal boundary should close back into the courtesy lane rather than float as a promoted utility family.
If the decline does not land or the follow-up becomes hard to parse, the user needs the direct repair branch immediately.

deferredBoundaryFamilies.note: Visible deferred boundary only; do not count this family inside the promoted starter cluster count.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: The cleanest next move is usually to ask the speaker to slow down.
If the conversation still is not landing, the later-hold call-for-me branch is the stronger fallback.

familyRelations.reason: Slower speech is the fastest repair after admitting you do not understand.
Showing the pinned place is a practical recovery when the hard part is the destination itself.
Calling for help is useful but intentionally parked behind stronger pickup rows.

clusters.familyTitle: Can you speak a little slower?

possibleTravelerResponses.note: A respectful acknowledgment is a likely short close after the speaker slows down.

familyRelations.reason: This family is the natural follow-up to the direct understanding repair.
A brief acknowledgment is the cleanest close once the pace improves.
If the missing piece is a place name, the parked map-showing line is still the better fallback.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: Once the driver understands the destination, the next high-value move is often telling them where to stop.

familyRelations.reason: Destination and drop-off control are the two most practical ride-control moves in the current starter slice.
If the driver starts speaking too quickly, the repair lane matters immediately.
The parked map-showing line solves the same destination problem in a more explicit way.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: Thank you is the cleanest close once the stop lands.

familyRelations.reason: This family usually appears on the same ride-control surface as the destination line.
The travel exchange usually closes with a quick thank you.
Cash remains a visible but intentionally parked payment follow-on.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: If the first option is not right, the next likely move is to ask for another one.
If the price works, the traveler often moves straight to the commit phrase.

familyRelations.reason: Comparison shopping is the most common next move after the first price quote.
The current starter slice needs a clean branch from quote to purchase commit.
Card payment stays visible as a parked follow-on without being promoted yet.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: A quick price check is the most likely next move for this convenience-store ask.

familyRelations.reason: Water purchase utility improves when the traveler can move directly into price confirmation.
Hydration and spice-control often live on the same practical food-and-drink surface.
Card payment is the nearby parked follow-on once the stronger pickup set is reopened.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: Takeaway is a practical next branch once the food preference is clear.

familyRelations.reason: Takeaway is one of the most practical nearby food actions in the current starter slice.
Water is a nearby comfort need even though it lives in a store context instead of a stall context.
If the server starts explaining ingredients or options too quickly, the repair lane matters immediately.

youMayHearSignals.signalText: Just a few minutes, maybe walkable.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: If the answer is vague, the next useful question is usually how long the walk will take.

familyRelations.reason: Near-here and walk-time checks belong on the same direction-repair surface.
If the place is not near, the traveler often jumps from walking to ride-control.
The parked map-showing line is the cleaner fallback if naming the place is the real blocker.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: If the walk is too long or unclear, the traveler often jumps straight to ride-control.

familyRelations.reason: Walking versus riding is the main decision boundary this family feeds.
A simple thank you is the cleanest close once the answer is clear.
If the timing explanation is too fast or too detailed, the traveler needs the repair lane.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: Price-checking is one of the most common questions that follows a polite opener.
The parked map-showing line is the best next-step candidate when the need is finding a place.

familyRelations.reason: Shopping questions are a common follow-up after this polite opener.
Map-showing remains the most useful parked follow-on after a polite attention-getter.
If the conversation starts moving too quickly right after the opener, the repair lane is the safest fallback.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: A respectful acknowledgment is a likely short reply after thanks.
If the moment is really a polite decline rather than a thank-you close, keep the refusal row visible as a deferred boundary only.

familyRelations.reason: A short respectful acknowledgment is a plausible reply after the traveler says thank you.
The deferred refusal line stays visible as a boundary case, not as a promoted courtesy family.

youMayHearSignals.signalText: Wait here, we can call someone.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: The traveler may still need a polite attention-getter before delivering the urgent request.
Call-for-me remains the visible escalation branch, but it stays parked until stronger rows are cleared.

familyRelations.reason: Getting attention politely still matters in many non-emergency medical situations.
If the response is too fast or too detailed, the user still needs the direct repair branch.
Calling someone for the traveler is useful but intentionally kept in the later-only hold lane.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: Thank you is the cleanest close once the takeaway request is understood.

familyRelations.reason: Food preference and takeaway live on the same practical food surface.
A quick thank you usually closes the food-counter exchange.
The purchase-commit family is a nearby utility branch when the traveler is deciding whether to take the item.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: Once the better option is visible, the next likely move is to commit to it.

familyRelations.reason: Comparison shopping still sits under the same price-check surface.
The current sample should let the traveler move cleanly from comparison to commit.
Payment method stays visible as the next parked follow-on once the stronger pickup set is reopened.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: A quick thank you is the cleanest close once the purchase decision is made.

familyRelations.reason: Many short purchase exchanges simply close with thanks once the item is chosen.
Card payment remains the most relevant parked follow-on after a purchase commit.
The traveler often jumps between commit and renewed price clarification in the same browse surface.

possibleTravelerResponses.note: After a respectful acknowledgment, the conversation often closes with thanks.
If the moment shifts from acknowledgment into a polite decline, keep the refusal row visible as a deferred boundary only.

familyRelations.reason: Respectful acknowledgment and thanks are the two main courtesy closers in the current starter slice.
If the traveler nodded along but still is lost, the repair branch must stay nearby.
The refusal row is a visible boundary neighbor, but it stays outside the promoted courtesy cluster.
